channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In the campaign's final weeks, Harris is keeping a focus on 'blue wall' states
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: Asian American evangelical Christians are an evolving group of voters who are increasingly seeking to distinguish themselves from their white counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other service workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Vulnerable Dem senator tied to 'radical groups' working to close massive power plant in swing state
item.description: Vulnerable Democratic Sen. Sherrod Brown has partnered with multiple groups that are attempting to shut down the largest coal plant in the state of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Epic clash: Pressed by Fox News, Kamala Harris comes out swinging
item.description: In her first interview on Fox News, Kamala Harris ducked questions, raised her voice and appeared to be trying to project an image of standing up to both Trump and the network.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Harris' interview with Fox News is marked by testy exchanges over immigration and more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris engaged in a combative interview with Fox News on Wednesday, sparring with anchor Bret Baier on immigration and shifting policy positions while asserting that if elected, she would not represent a continuation of Joe Biden's presidency.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is California's Proposition 5 on votes for bond measures?
item.description: Nearly every election cycle, Californians vote on a measures to allow local governments to borrow money in the form of bonds. Under state law, however, it is quite hard for these measures to actually pass — something that could change in November.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: “Lyft believes transportation access should never be a barrier for any citizen seeking to vote," Lyft Chief Policy Officer Jerry Golden said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics: Ex-Vegas pol gets life in the slammer
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border Patrol facing large-scale surveillance camera outage with ‘significant impacts’: report
item.description: Border Patrol agents are struggling with a reported drop of nearly a third of their surveillance cameras, according to a new report released this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: FEMA leader: Misinformation about hurricane operations must stop
item.description: "The ongoing spread of misinformation about operations is not OK," FEMA Administrator Deanne Criswell said.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Harris leading Trump by 5 points among likely voters: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris is leading former President Trump by 5 points nationally among likely voters, according to a Marist Poll survey released Wednesday. Harris and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), received 52 percent support, while Trump and his running mate, Sen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mormon voters set to play critical role in swing states
item.description: Mormon voters are increasingly seen as a crucial swing vote in the presidential election, with both Vice President Harris and former President Trump stepping up their outreach to the demographic in multiple battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Harris campaign hosts separated families ahead of Trump's Univision town hall
item.description: The Harris campaign has mostly centered its immigration and border security messaging on enforcement, drawing some criticism from advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany this week, Angola in December for visits delayed by Hurricane Milton
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is heading to Germany Thursday and will visit Angola the first week in December, rescheduling visits that were postponed so he could remain in Washington to monitor the federal response to Hurricane Milton as it struck Florida.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Liz Cheney jumps into House races — for Democrats
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) is taking her anti-Trump crusade to the congressional battlefield, endorsing Democrats in a pair of contested House races in a sign that she sees the lower chamber as a potential check on former President Trump if he wins a second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Biden is out campaigning again — but not for her husband anymore. She's pumping up Harris
item.description: CLAWSON, Mich. (AP) — Jill Biden wasted no time after she stepped up to the microphone at a suburban Detroit restaurant. “Now some have come to (the) Detroit area recently and thrown around some insults, but from what I’ve seen this is a vibrant, thriving city,” she said.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iowa emerges as key battleground in race for the House
item.description: In their fight to flip the House, Democrats are eyeing gains in what may seem an unlikely spot: Iowa. Two GOP seats in the Hawkeye State have emerged as true battlegrounds in the final stretch of the campaign, as some election forecasters have moved Reps.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Harris is pressed on the concerns of Black voters in Charlamagne tha God town hall
item.description: Harris’ town hall with the influential radio host came at a crucial moment in the campaign, as polls show her struggling to cement the level of support she may need from Black men to win the White House.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Harris brings a bit of Trump into her rally to try to show him as ‘unhinged’
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris wants voters to pay more attention when Trump talks about “the enemy within.” On Monday, she played a highlight reel to paint him as “increasingly unstable and unhinged.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Tim Scott mulls run to lead Senate Republicans' campaign arm
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott (R-S.C.) is seriously considering making a run to become chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) in 2026, sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Harris helps pack aid boxes for North Carolina hurricane victims ahead of Sunday campaign push
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday helped pack diapers into boxes of personal care products destined for North Carolina hurricane victims, agreeing with one helper who said “it takes a village.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5
